output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 3/3.
Batch 1: Implemented batch 1 task T1 by adding the memoized shot-grouping hook and deterministic shot colors, then verified it with a clean TypeScript pass. Wrote a batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/shot-bounding-boxes-on-20260406-0333/execution_batch_1.json`.
Batch 2: Completed batch 2. Added the `useShotGroups` hook test file and wired memoized shot bounding-box overlays into `TimelineEditor`/`TimelineCanvas`, including drag/resize hiding and hover-only shot-name strips. Verification passed for `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts`, and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/shot-bounding-boxes-on-20260406-0333/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Completed batch 3 verification. The temporary repro test confirmed `TimelineCanvas` renders a shot hover strip for adjacent same-shot clips and hides it during drag, then the repro script was deleted. The planned verification commands all passed: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx`, and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/shot-bounding-boxes-on-20260406-0333/execution_batch_3.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Created `useShotGroups.ts` with a memoized `useShotGroups` hook, `ShotGroup` type, and deterministic 8-color `getShotColor`. The hook resolves clip generation IDs through `meta[clipId].asset -> registry.assets[assetKey].generationId`, skips missing links without throwing, pre-sorts row actions once, and emits only 2+ clip groups with increasing shot-image order and `gap < 0.5s`. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.

Added `useShotGroups.test.ts` with 8 renderHook/unit cases covering adjacent same-track grouping, oversized gaps, wrong image order, mixed-shot splitting, single-clip rejection, cross-track rejection, deterministic sample colors, and missing-generation handling. Verified with `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts`.

Wired `useShotGroups` into `TimelineEditor` and added memoized positioned shot-box overlays in `TimelineCanvas`. The render layer re-validates adjacency with `SNAP_THRESHOLD_PX / pixelsPerSecond`, applies `resizeOverrides`, hides overlays during drag or resize, and keeps the hover strip confined to the 4px top margin above clips. Verified with `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts` and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.

Ran the full planned verification set with no code changes required during this batch. `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts` passed, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx` passed, and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. Also ran a temporary repro test that mounted `TimelineCanvas`, confirmed the shot hover strip appears for adjacent same-shot clips, and confirmed it disappears during drag; the temporary script was deleted after the run.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the hook uses the required resolution chain `meta[action.id]?.asset -> registry.assets[assetKey]?.generationId` and silently excludes clips whenever any lookup in that chain is missing.
Covered both required edge cases explicitly: one test asserts no grouping for a single matching clip, and another asserts no grouping when same-shot clips are split across two tracks.

The overlay gate is `dragSessionRef.current !== null || Object.keys(resizeOverrides).length > 0`, so boxes hide during drag and resize. The only pointer-active element is the 4px hover strip placed at `top = group.top - ACTION_VERTICAL_MARGIN`, which keeps it in the margin above the clip body instead of over the drag target.

Yes. The existing timeline-related test modules passed without modification: `ClipAction.test.tsx`, `useClipEditing.test.ts`, and `DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx` all passed alongside the new `useShotGroups.test.ts`, and TypeScript verification also passed.
